internal_project_documents.note: Tracked inventory of scripts and canonical output paths (always in git).
Unified TSV conventions: separate has_image_ref from image_download_ok / training-ready; multimodal cohort filtering expectations.
Optional local notes (gitignored by default): cohort fetch scope, validity-score threshold, training eligibility decisions.
Optional local thesis/proposal (gitignored by default): SMART goals; risks on broken/missing images.
Authoritative implementation of the heuristic validity score and buckets.
Per-image audit trail (score, flags, entropy, variance, optional phash).

dataset_sources_thesis.cite_in_proposal: Shu et al. (as in msc_proposal.tex / project bibliography)
Nakamura et al. (as in msc_proposal.tex / project bibliography)

references.literature_context_note: Multimodal misinformation papers often report filtering missing text/image pairs, broken URLs, or obviously unusable media when building social benchmarks; they do not standardise this project's composite entropy/aspect/resolution score. Treat this heuristic as an explicit, reproducible engineering rule for this dissertation pipeline.
references.illustrative_related_work: Recent work sometimes emphasises noisy or incomplete social-media multimodal inputs (e.g. Srivastava et al., 2025, CLIP-based detection in noisy environments — ICONAT); that line of work motivates rigorous data handling but is not the source of the validity-score formula.
